errorBoundary.titulo: Something went wrong in this tool
errorBoundary.mensagemRede: Connection error — the tool could not be loaded. Check your internet and reload.

busca.semResultadosDica: Try different terms or remove the active filters.

lisp-calculator.descricao: Evaluate mathematical expressions using LISP (prefix) notation.

circuito-eletrico.descricao: Calculate currents in electrical circuits using matrix inversion and Kirchhoff's Law.

calculadora-juros.descricao: Calculate simple or compound interest with a period-by-period evolution table.

estatistica-descritiva.descricao: Calculate mean, median, mode, standard deviation and other metrics from a list of numbers.

conversor-moeda.descricao: Convert values between the world's major currencies with daily updated rates.

conversor-bases.descricao: Convert numbers between binary, octal, decimal and hexadecimal.

operacoes-matrizes.descricao: Add, multiply, transpose and calculate determinants of 2x2 and 3x3 matrices.

resolvedor-triangulos.descricao: Calculate sides, angles, area and perimeter using the Law of Sines and the Law of Cosines.

calculadora-progressoes.descricao: Calculate the general term, sum and sequence of arithmetic (AP) and geometric (GP) progressions.

conversor-unidades.descricao: Convert between different units of length, area, volume, mass, temperature and time.

lispCalculator.erroComparacao: Calculate the first expression before comparing.

complexos.desc: Use the format a+bj ("j" represents the imaginary unit). Some valid examples:

funcoes.raiz: raiz(x) → square root of x (works with negatives)

variaveis.desc: Any letter that is not conj, raiz or j is treated as a variable — a field to fill in the value appears automatically before calculating. Example: a + b * 2.

output.tokens: tokens — the expression broken into pieces
output.posfixa: postfix — internal order used to calculate
output.lisp: lisp — the same expression in prefix notation, e.g.: (+ 3 4j)

comparacao.desc: The "compare with another expression" button checks if two expressions have the same structure (same operators in the same order) — not whether they give the same numerical result.

erros.operadorSemOperandos: Malformed expression: not enough operands for operator
erros.zeroPotenciaNegativa: Cannot raise zero to a negative power

erros.camposVazios: Fill in all fields before calculating.
erros.numerosInvalidos: One or more values are not valid numbers.
erros.semSolucao: Malformed circuit (system has no unique solution).

oQueE.desc: Solves a system of 3 linear equations (Kirchhoff's Voltage Law) using 3x3 matrix inversion, finding the electrical current in each mesh of a circuit.

comoPreencher.desc: For each mesh, enter the three resistances (R) that relate that mesh to currents I1, I2 and I3, and the applied voltage (V). Resistance values must be greater than zero.

resultado.desc: Each current (I1, I2, I3) can be positive or negative: positive means the current flows in the initially assumed direction, negative indicates the opposite direction. The verification substitutes the results back into the original equations to confirm calculation accuracy.

erros.camposVazios: Fill in all fields before calculating.
erros.numerosInvalidos: One or more values are not valid numbers.
erros.valoresPositivos: Capital, rate and periods must be greater than zero.
erros.periodosInvalidos: The number of periods must be an integer and at most 600.

ajuda.titulo: How to use the interest calculator

tipos.desc: Simple interest grows linearly — the interest for each period is always calculated on the initial capital. Compound interest grows exponentially — each period's interest is calculated on the accumulated amount (interest on interest).

campos.desc: Enter the initial capital (invested amount), the interest rate per period (in %) and the number of periods. Select the unit (month or year) — when switching, periods are automatically converted to keep the equivalent duration.

taxa.desc: Below the result, the calculator shows the equivalent rate in the other unit. For compound interest, the conversion uses the correct exponential formula — for example, 2% per month is not 24% per year, but approximately 26.82% per year.

tabela.desc: Shows the amount and accumulated interest at the end of each period, allowing you to track the growth of the investment over time.

descriptiveStats.placeholder: Paste numbers here: 10, 20, 30 or one per line

entrada.desc: Paste or type numbers separated by comma, space, semicolon or line break. Maximum of 1000 values at a time.

metricas.mediana: Median — middle value of sorted data
metricas.moda: Mode — value(s) that appear most frequently
metricas.desvioPadrao: Std Deviation — spread around the mean
metricas.amplitude: Range — difference between max and min

moda.desc: If all values appear exactly the same number of times, the distribution has no mode — the tool displays "No mode" in these cases.

erros.rede: Could not fetch the exchange rate. Check your connection.

comoUsar.desc: Enter the amount, select the source and destination currencies. The result appears automatically once the rate is loaded.

cotacao.desc: Rates are provided by ExchangeRate-API and updated once a day. These are reference market rates — they may differ slightly from rates offered by banks and exchange houses.

cache.desc: After the first fetch, the rate is saved locally for 24 hours. If you open the tool again within that period, the result appears instantly even without internet.

baseConverter.verPassos: + show base {{base}} conversion step by step

ajuda.titulo: How to use the base converter

oQueE.desc: A number base defines how many different symbols exist. Base 10 uses 0-9 (decimal, what we use daily). Base 2 uses 0 and 1 (binary, used in computers). Base 8 uses 0-7 (octal). Base 16 uses 0-9 and A-F (hexadecimal, common in programming and colors).

comoUsar.desc: Select the base of the number you have, enter the value and click Convert. The result appears simultaneously in all 4 bases. For hexadecimal, letters A-F are accepted in upper or lowercase.

passos.desc: For non-decimal bases, you can expand the conversion steps — the successive division algorithm shows how to reach the result, useful for learning or checking the reasoning.

operacoes.somar: A + B — element-wise addition (matrices must have the same size)
operacoes.transposta: Transpose — rows become columns and vice versa
operacoes.determinante: Determinant — scalar that summarizes matrix properties

multiplicacao.desc: Matrix multiplication is not commutative: A×B is generally different from B×A. The element at row i, column j of the result is the dot product of row i of A with column j of B.

determinante.desc: A determinant equal to zero means the matrix is singular — it has no inverse and the associated linear system has no unique solution.

ajuda.titulo: How to use the unit converter

comoUsar.desc: Select the category, choose the source unit in the selector next to the field, and enter the value. All equivalents appear instantly, without clicking any button.

temperatura.desc: Temperature conversion uses direct formulas (not simple multiplication): °C × 9/5 + 32 = °F, and °C + 273.15 = K. The converter applies this automatically.

precisao.desc: Results are displayed with up to 6 significant digits. For very large or very small values, scientific notation is used automatically.

erros.camposVazios: Fill in all fields before calculating.
erros.numerosInvalidos: One or more values are not valid numbers.
erros.nInvalido: Number of terms must be an integer between 1 and 100.
erros.vazioSeq: Enter a sequence of numbers to identify.
erros.minimoTermos: Enter at least 2 terms to identify.

output.nenhuma: The sequence is neither AP nor GP.

ajuda.titulo: How to use the progression calculator

pa.desc: An AP has a constant difference between consecutive terms (common difference d). Enter the first term (a₁), the common difference and the number of terms to calculate the last term, sum and full sequence.

pg.desc: A GP has a constant ratio between consecutive terms (common ratio r). Works like AP but uses multiplication instead of addition. The ratio cannot be zero.

identificar.desc: Paste a sequence of numbers separated by comma, space or semicolon. The tool automatically checks if it is AP, GP, both (like 1, 1, 1) or neither, and shows the corresponding ratio.
